evals.prompt: I need a safe automation plan for provisioning a new SharePoint site and applying repeatable configuration across environments. I am not sure when to use PnP PowerShell, CLI for Microsoft 365, or site scripts.
evals.expected_output: Uses tool-selection, auth, provisioning, and idempotency guidance with clear boundaries.

evals.prompt: Help me modernize a SharePoint admin script now that the old shared PnP Management Shell app is gone. I need to register my own Entra ID app and choose the right connection flow for PnP PowerShell.
evals.expected_output: Uses auth and PnP PowerShell guidance based on the current registration reality.

evals.prompt: We have an SPFx solution plus some automation around it. Can you help me decide what should live in PnPjs inside the project versus what should be handled by PnP PowerShell or CLI for Microsoft 365 in deployment scripts?
evals.expected_output: Uses cross-tool boundaries and SPFx integration guidance instead of collapsing everything into one tool.

evals.prompt: I need a repeatable SharePoint Online script: connect with Connect-PnPOnline using app-only auth, then run m365 spo commands for tenant checks. Which tool should own each part?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes command-level trigger language, separates PnP PowerShell versus CLI for Microsoft 365 responsibilities, and preserves auth plus automation boundaries.

evals.prompt: Build an SPFx web part with React property panes and Fluent UI theme tokens. If this package is not the right owner, route to the correct MartiX SharePoint skill.
assertions: States whether the request is inside this skill boundary before applying rules.
Does not load unrelated rule files or claim ownership of another skill domain.
Recommends martix-sharepoint-spfx when the request is SPFx component implementation.

evals.prompt: I need a SharePoint farm solution with Feature Framework receivers, WSP packaging, and master page deployment. If this package is not the right owner, route to the correct MartiX SharePoint skill.
assertions: States that classic server-side SharePoint artifacts are outside this package boundary.
Does not answer as though PnP tooling owns WSP or Feature Framework implementation.
Recommends martix-sharepoint-server when the request is classic farm-solution work.
